mnemonics: Always Positive & Sums to One (APSO): A Probability Vector is Always Positive and Sums to One.
Think of a probability vector as a 'Share Pie' chart. Each slice is non-negative, and all slices together make up the whole pie (sum to 1).
keyPoints: A probability vector is a vector where each entry is a non-negative real number.
The sum of all entries (components) in a probability vector must equal 1.
They represent a probability distribution over a finite set of states or outcomes.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that probabilities must be non-negative (cannot be negative).
Creating a vector where the entries do not sum to exactly 1.
Confusing a probability vector with a general state vector that isn't normalized.
